___ now on adjuvant doxorubicin and cisplatin presenting for 
scheduled chemotherapy.

He was admitted to ___ on ___ for cycle 1 of doxorubicin 
and cisplatin. He had right chest wall port-a-cath placement and 
subsequently completed completed his first cycle without 
significant issue. He was discharged home on ___ and 
self-administered Neulasta on ___ without difficulty. He 
reports doing relatively well at home with the exception of 
diarrhea. He reports up to 3 watery, non-bloody bowel movements 
daily that started the day after discharge home. He took Imodium 
with mild relief. He reports taking Lomotil TID with good 
effect. His only other complaint was mild nose bleeds from the 
bilateral nostrils.  Episodes are generally self-limited and 
resolve within a few minutes. He has had no epistaxis for the 
last week prior to admission. He denies any other bleeding from 
gum, bowel, or
hematuria. He denies easy bruising.

He reports continued improvement in his right thigh/hip 
incisions. He discontinued narcotics and is on Tylenol PRN. 
Swelling is improving as

clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
abnormal labs
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none

History of Present Illness:
Mr. ___ is a ___ with high-grade unclassified sarcoma 
(malignant fibrous histiocytoma) of the right femur s/p radical 
resection on ___ now on adjuvant doxorubicin and cisplatin x 
4 cycles. He was last treated on ___. He received post 
chemo hydration and required repletion of electrolytes prior to 
discharge. He was sent home with instructions to continue oral 
magnesium supplements but due to side effects of diarrhea, 
stopped taking these.

He went for routine lab tests at an outside facility and was 
noted to have severe electrolyte depletion and acute kidney 
injury.

He is being admitted for expedient work up. 

Other than diarrhea, and easy fatigability he denies any other 
symptoms. He has had no sick contacts. He denies any nausea or 
vomiting but does have a poor appetite. He denies chest pain, 
shortness of breath, lightheadedness, cough, fevers/chills, 
abdominal pain, dysuria, frequency, or flank pain. Diarrhea is 
now resolved.
  
 
On arrival to the Floor, he is in no distress with stable vital 
signs.

Past Medical History:
PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY:
-___: Saw orthopedist for groin pain. X-ray showed a
concerning lesion in the right hip. He subsequently had an MRI
that was read as chondral lesion of the proximal femur.
-___: Referred to ___ Orthopedics. Repeat MRI on ___
showed a right femoral neck lesion, which appeared primarily
cystic but with an irregular thick and enhancing wall, along 
with
a pathologic fracture through the lesion. CT Chest and Bone Scan
on ___ were both negative for metastatic disease.
-___: Biopsy of the bony lesion showed scant fragments of
atypical epithelioid cellular proliferation involv
[... truncated ...]

Hospital Course:
This is a ___ year old male osteosarcoma currently on 
chemotherapy with doxorubicin and cisplatin presenting on Day 11 
of cycle 4 with acute kidney injury.

# ___: Presented with Cr of 1.9 from baseline of 1.1-1.2 back in 
___. Concern for platinum-related nephrotoxicity vs 
hypovolemia given recent diarrhea. Underwent bilateral renal 
ultrasound which ruled out post-obstructive process and revealed 
normal parenchyma. No recent NSAID or antibiotic use. Improved 
slowly with continuous IVF. FeNa calculated at 0.4% with no 
increased salt avidity on urine lytes. No volume overload on 
exam or concerning electrolyte derangements. Will re-check renal 
function next week with bi-weekly post-chemo CBCs to ensure 
recovery in renal function is sustained. 

# Sarcoma: Currently on palliative chemo regimen: 
Adriamycin/Cisplatin of C4d1 = ___. 
- further therapy as per primary oncologist, Dr. ___.

# Neutropenia: Patient arrived during nadir of recent 
chemotherapy. ANC of 340 on presentation, improved to 1700 on 
___. No fevers or infectious complications on this 
hospitalization.  Expected with recent chemo. No febrile 
episodes. No focal signs or sxs of infection.

# Anemia/thrombocytopenia: As above, expected consequence of 
recent cytotoxic chemotherapy. Patient required 2 units of pRBCs 
and 1 ___ with appropriate response to counts. Will 
need bi-weekly monitoring pending count recovery, which patient 
may be close to given recent recovery of ANC.  

# Hypertension: Atenolol
[... truncated ...]

Discharge Diagnosis (prior stay — from note):
primary: acute kidney injury; pancytopenia secondary to 
cytotoxic chemotherapy. 

secondary: high-grade malignant fibrous histioc
[... truncated ...]

___ I&D right thigh hematoma

History of Present Illness:
Per Dr. ___:
This is a ___ with high grade sarcoma s/p resection of the
proximal femur with proximal femur replacement and adjuvant
cisplatin doxorubicin who presented to clinic today complaining
of worsening exertional dyspnea over the past week as well as
left-sided lower chest pain, worsened with deep inspiration. A
stat CTA ordered in clinic showed "Extensive acute bilateral
lobar and segmental pulmonary emboli, with near occlusive emboli
in the lower lobe lobar branches and nonocclusive emboli in the
segmental branches. The main right and left pulmonary arteries
are patent, with no evidence of right heart strain. Left lateral
lower lobe wedge-shaped opacity, may reflect atelectasis versus
early infarct. Small left pleural effusion and adjacent
atelectasis." He was sent to the ED for evaluation. 

In the ED, initial vital signs were 98.4 90 125/70 18 99% RA. A

Hospital Course:
=============================
Brief summary statement:
.
___ y/o M with PMHx of R femur sarcoma (malignant, fibrous, 
histiocytoma type s/p radical resection ___ with adjuvant 
doxorubicin and cisplatin completed in ___, HTN, 
neuropathy, former smoker, who presented with exertional dyspnea 
and was found to have bilateral PEs as well as anemia; now found 
to have a large femoral hematoma.  
==============================
Hospital course by problem:
.
# RLE Hematoma:
Noted on MRI ___. Possibly post-surgical with 
acute-on-chronic component, given ongoing anemia. Likely 
worsened with anticoagulation for new PE (below). DDx includes 
small tumorrecurrence (esp given pelvic LAD) predisposing pt to 
bleeding. Per ___ no drainable collection and no active bleed on 
CTA. Pt was taken for surgery by Dr. ___ on ___ where 
hematoma was evacuated and pathology sent and wound vac placed. 
He was taken back to the OR on ___ but case was quickly eneded 
following an aspiration event. He went back to the OR on ___ 
for closure, with two JP drains placed.  JP drains removed on 
___.  He was evaluated by ___ several times during his 
hospitalization, they ultimately recommended discharge home with 
outpatient ___ visits.  He currently requires use of ambulatory 
assist device (RW or AC).
.
# Fever:
On ___. No clear source. Possibly related to PE vs. much 
less likely superinfection of hematoma vs. less likely GI 
infection, (diarrhea resolved, c diff negative). Resolved 
spontaneously. 

#

He has had extensive history of complications from his RLE 
sarcoma, including multiple hematomas requiring surgical 
intervention. He last had in ___ right leg hematoma which 
required surgical evacuation. This hospitalization was c/b 
submassive PE and IVC was placed. Path showed recurrent sarcoma. 
___ were negative for occult bleed. Given anemia, 
hematology was consulted and patient was discharged on lower 
dose of enoxaparin (1mg/kg daily). Patient was then discharged 
home, but continued to require transfusions. Patient was offered 
palliative amputation but he declined. Given ongoing anemia 
requiring transfusions, enoxaparin was discontinued and patient 
was started on palliative XRT and consideration of palliative 
chemotherapy in the future.

recurrence), stage III CKD, PE (not on AC), anemia (most recent 
baseline Hgb ___ who was admitted with severe anemia.

# Acute on Chronic Anemia: Patient was admitted to ___ 
after being found to have significant anemia with H/H 4.4/14.5 
while at his palliative XRT session. Patient's anemia was likely 
due to bleeding into hematoma of RLE. Hematoma size previously 
noted to be 58-59cm in largest diameter. Patient had no evidence 
of gastrointestinal blood loss, with guaiac neg at OSH. Patient 
received transfusion of 6u PRBCs, and was HDS after this. 
Patient had no evidence of hemolysis on labs. He was transferred 
to ___ and was assessed by orthopedic oncology, who noted that 
no urgent intervention was indicated in the setting of 
hemodynamic stability. Vascular surgery was consulted, who noted 
mild swelling of the thigh with soft compartment, which possibly 
represented recurrent disease or hematoma, with all pulses 
palpable and no compromise of extremities. Vascular surgery 
stated that there was no acute indication for intervention at 
this time and they recommended observation and ___ consult for 
embolization if an acute bleed were to develop. Repeat CT lower 
extremity was notable for no definitive active bleeding but 
evidence of extensive hematoma involving the right lateral thigh 
to at mid femur. During ___ hospital stay, his Hgb/Hct 
were

and receiving local XRT, who p/w recurrent anemia (Hg 4) in 
clinic. He presented to his clinic w/ significant dyspnea, 
appeared pale. He had his Hg checked it was 5. He was 
transferred to ___ and transferred to ___ oncology 
service. His repeat Hg was 4 and admitted for transfusions. He 
had denied any increase in size of his thigh hematoma. He had a 
CT scan which revealed enlarging thigh hematoma but no active 
extravasation.

Past Medical History:
-___: Patient noted right groin pain that progressed over the
following weeks.
-___: X-ray with his orthopedist that showed a concerning
lesion in the right hip.
-___: MRI hip showed a pathologic fracture of the right
femoral neck due to a large lesion in the proximal femur. This
was thought to be most consistent with potentially a chondral
lesion of the proximal femur.
-___: Referred to ___ Orthopedics. Repeat MRI on ___
showed a right femoral neck lesion, which appeared primarily
cystic but with an irregular thick and enhancing wall, along 
with
a pathologic fracture through the lesion. CT C
[... truncated ...]

Hospital Course:
___ w/ CKD III, high-grade unclassified sarcoma (malignant 
fibrous histiocytoma) of the R femur s/p radical resection on 
___, s/p adjuvant doxorubicin and cisplatin (completed 
___, c/b b/l extensive PE (dx ___, found to have local 
recurrence and pulmonary nodules, c/b by recurrent hematoma, now 
s/p IVC and off anticoagulation and local XRT, who p/w recurrent 
anemia (Hg 4), found to have enlarging thigh hematoma.

# Severe Hypoproliferative Anemia
# Thigh Hematoma, Chronic

Etiology presumed to be his thigh hematoma which developed on 
initiation of anticoagulation. He is s/p XRT to the thigh w/ 
improvement in the girth and pain. Repeat CTA here revealed no 
active extravasation but definite increased size of the 
hematoma. He likely is slowly oozing into it and hence difficult 
to intervene on apart from a radical amputation. In addition, he 
seems to be holding on to his transfusions only for a short 
period of time, that is just barely a week. He likely has a poor 
erythropoietic response due to XRT, malignant involvement of the 
bone, and prior chemo. MAHA unlikely w/ normal bili, hapto, LDH, 
hence not TTP. However in context of elevated INR, hematology 
oncology has been consulted. Bone marrow biopsy was done on 
___. abd US on ___ did not reveal any sequestration. 
Hematology felt that he did not have a consumptive process, nor 
hemolysis, and bone marrow revealed no signs of leukemia. 
However a myelodysplastic process has not been entirely ruled 
out, nor myelo
[... truncated ...]

Discharge Diagnosis (prior stay — from note):
High-grade unclassified sarcoma (malignant fibrous histiocytoma) 
of the R femur
Hematoma, chronic
Hypoproliferative Anemia
Vitamin B12 Deficiency
Elevated Prothrombin Time
Thrombocytopenia
History of bilateral PE and DVT
Hypomagnesemia
Hyponatremia
Hypoalbuminemia
Stage II Pressure Ulcer with deep tissue injury

clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
Pulmonary Embolism
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None.

History of Present Illness:
Mr. ___ is ___ man with a high-grade unclassified 
sarcoma (malignant fibrous histiocytoma) of the right femur s/p 
radical resection in ___ s/p adjuvant doxorubicin and 
cisplatin x 4 cycles completed ___, now with recently 
diagnosed recurrence in the pelvis and likely in the lungs. 
Course complicated by recurrent hemorrhage into the right leg 
sarcoma/hematoma. He is admitted from the ED with new incidental 
finding of PE on imaging.
Patient underwent routine staging CT of chest and lower 
extremity day of admission, along with MRI of L-Spine. On CT of 
chest, he was noted to have new extensive left sided PE, which 
was reported to Dr. ___ by the radiologist and he was sent 
to the ED.
In the ED, initial VS were pain 0, T 98.1, HR 100, BP 99/59, RR 
18, O2 100%RA. Labs were notable for: WBC 9.3, HCT 25.0, PLT 
110, Na 133, K 3.9, HCO3 29, Cr 0.8, Trop negative x1. Repeat 
HCT down to 22.8.Patient was started on IV heparin gtt with 
bolus prior to transfer for further management. VS prior to 
transfer were T 98.6, HR 96, BP 99/63, RR 18, O2 100%RA.
On arrival to the floor, patient has no acute complaint. Denies 
chest pain or shortness of breath. No cough. No fevers or 
chills. 
[... truncated ...]

the following weeks.

femoral neck due to a large lesion in the proximal femur. This 
was thought to be most consistent with potentially a chondral 
lesion of the proximal femur.

showed a right femoral neck lesion, which appeared primarily 
cystic but with an irregular thick and enhancing wall, along 
with a pathologic fracture through
[... truncated ...]

Hospital Course:
Mr. ___ is ___ man with a high-grade unclassified 
sarcoma (malignant fibrous histiocytoma)
of the right femur s/p radical resection in ___ s/p adjuvant 
doxorubicin and cisplatin x 4 cycles completed ___, now with 
recently diagnosed recurrence in the pelvis and likely in the 
lungs with recurrent hemorrhage into the right leg 
sarcoma/hematoma and previous PE who presented with new 
incidental finding of PE on imaging. 

Pulmonary Embolism: 
- Patient with prior extensive bilateral PE diagnosed in ___ and IVC filter was placed in ___ given his ongoing 
hemorrhage into right thigh hematoma while on lovenox before 
ultimately stopping the lovenox due to persistent bleeding. 
Patient with incidental finding of large PE on follow-up 
imaging. He has been asymptomatic. He was briefly started on IV 
heparin but that was stopped. Troponins negative with mildly 
elevated BNP and no evidence of right heart strain on CT or EKG. 
He is high risk for bleeding while on anticoagulation due to 
ongoing uncontrolled bleeding and concern for compartment 
syndrome in the right lower extremity. Amputation was discussed 
to control ongoing bleeding at sarcoma site which would then 
allow for anticoagulation but this was determined to be a high 
risk in the setting of PEs and unclear benefit given decreased 
life expectancy and the decision was made by orthopedic surgery, 
the patient's primary oncologist and the patient to not proceed. 
The patient does have an IVC filter which placement was 

[...

Osseous structures are unremarkable apart from a right hip hemiarthroplasty.

small right cerebellar lesion.  Evaluate for metastatic disease.
